item.content:encoded: This is officially the highest number of upvotes I've ever gotten! Thanks everyone!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You may be right about that! I think I'm keeping it as-is for now, but if you have anything specific in mind for how I could expand it I'd be happy to hear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this needs a little more buildup if it's to be an effective shaggy dog story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I generally agree with this, even after the update. It’s a character work, but the characters aren’t cleaned out.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the SCP Foundation even takes calling out sick when you aren't sick to the next level, huh? I should have guessed lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well this is a bit awkward .-.

On a real note, I really like this scp, it gives something new other than the usual thing that kills you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how I feel about this as an SCP, but I can't deny the charm this short work has. +1 from me, good work.

item.title: UPDATE
item.content:encoded:
I may be making some changes to 5097 in the future. For now, I've changed up some sentences and the final addendum, but more may be to come.

Also removed the first footnote.

item.title: Re: Sorry but the last sentence...?
item.content:encoded: It doesn't actually refer to a meme, this is just a little silly attempt at humor.

item.title: Sorry but the last sentence...?
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry, but today I cannot seem to find the will to even begin to can, therefore under these circumstances I willn't

I can not get it，literally. is this ‘can’ means ‘can be’， or is there any specific meme we need to know to understand this？

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey there! I get the feeling like we are of similar mindsets. Please feel free to message me to crit anything you write in the future!

Thank you! ^^

I think the general idea is genius.

Ah, thank you again! I'm glad you enjoyed this. I'll consider trying to flesh it out more in the future, but most people seem to like the shortness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey there! I get the feeling like we are of similar mindsets. Please feel free to message me to crit anything you write in the future!

I really enjoyed this. I have some of the same reservations others do about not quite being fleshed out, but I do like the abruptness, and I think the general idea is genius.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is odd to see, because as I see it, this is about a person so thoroughly crushed by life they couldn't bear living anymore. I thought the note was meant to be sorrowful, rather than comedic. I understand why you might think that, though, and I think that speaks to a lack of clear tone and atmosphere that this badly needs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The note is poised as some incredibly heart-wrenching moment, but, without any prior knowledge of who this person is or why they feel this way, it fails quite thoroughly to do this. I know next to nothing about this person, beyond the fact that they can't cope with life. It is too much to ask that we empathise with someone, who is, for all intents and purposes, not yet a character. Short-form works like this can be very successful, but this lacks what makes them good; this doesn't say a lot in a few words. It says "A researcher ceased to exist because she was no longer able to cope with life" and very little more. Where other short works like SCP-3637 may give a simple premise like this and imply a larger narrative, there is no bigger picture to see here.

-1

edit: If that line was meant as a joke, rather than as a sad moment, I feel this is worse. It's a single reference to a stale meme that was never funny without any real build-up or development.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love me a short article, and I will defend funny SCP stories on the Mainlist all day long.

But.

As much as the ConProcs gave me solid Douglas Adams vibes and had me gunnning for it, as much as the over dramatic verbiage of the Addendum made the meme land for me, the fact that this person/character exists in no way that we can relate to causes this to be a no-vote for me.

They stopped existing, so we can see that they’re gone. But they didn’t completely erase themself, so we can notice they are gone. But there is no detail about why they couldn’t even.

If they were gone so completely that we couldn’t see, there’s no article.

If they aren’t gone that completely, we should know some more about them. As to why they couldn’t even.

Hope this made sense!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't get me wrong, execution and concept were flawless. What you did here was clever!

But it just seems out of place in the mainlist to me. Sure, there are humorous SCPs with short and sweet punchlines such as this, but this is a liiiiiittle too referential and straight-to-the-point for it to gel in that way.

That said, in its current form it would make an excellent -J.

-1 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn’t work in its current form. The length isn’t the issue. The tone, a lack of context, and the total dearth of specific detail are the problems. From the very first line, “ In the event SCP-5097 returns to existence, it is to be severely reprimanded and placed in a humanoid containment cell.” it’s not clear what we’re supposed to be feeling. Whatever Olf values are I can’t help but feeling we’re getting into Humes all over again.

There’s no characterization beyond that cryptic note, which is done well enough but it’s being expected to carry the entirety of the article on its back page and just isn’t doing it. The effort level just isn’t here. This feels like the bare minimum of writing necessary to write out the concept but before the development process was really thought through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the catch, will fix it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most minorest of errors, note two:

Their reports on multiple anomalies were detected to be missing shortly after, followed their SCiPnet account becoming invalid.

I'm guessing its supposed to read "followed by their".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sometimes it really do be like that. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really sucks you didn't enjoy it. If I can run it by a few critters, I might extend the description a bit to give them more character? My only issue is that I don't want to bong down the reader with unneeded details and meander away from the main theme of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The lack of any semblance of real characterisation here meant I didn't care for this individual at all, meaning the only emotion I got out of the final note was disappointment at what is essentially a reference to a thoroughly unfunny meme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MaliceAforethought uses Item №/Anomaly Class sometimes.

My formatting was actually inspired by them! Real talk, I have slightly OCD-like tendencies from my autism, so sometimes things that aren't symmetrical bother me. So even if it wasn't Anomaly, it would've been Object #/Object Class or Item #/Item Class, unless I was using ACS.

Plus, it just feels fancier!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also why did you screw with the formatting here? It's not very significant here, but it also serves no purpose. Why not just use the normal format?

Authors generally develop signature methods of presenting articles that sometimes don't align with the usual Item #/Object Class layout. I know not_a_seagull uses their own subclasses fairly regularly in their articles and I think MaliceAforethought uses Item №/Anomaly Class sometimes.

It's a very slight deviation from the norm and it fits an author's headcanon. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The note at the end is weirdly poetic in a way that feels reminiscent of Emily Dickinson but it also feels a touch clunky? Specifically, the 'can' feels like it's too harsh a sounding word for that spot; and the phrase "therefore under these circumstances" feels like it really needs to be a touch more…casual? I think perhaps just the word length is screwing up the meter but I'm not a poet so shrugs

Oh, huh, I didn't think it was poetry. To be perfectly honest I have no idea what "meter" means, lol.

Possibly by fleshing out who Kara is as a person a bit more?

Originally there was a bit more character to them referenced in the document, but as the revisions went on I realized it wasn't entirely important to the story I wanted to tell. KaraKatt and Nagiros have both recently told me they're writing tales based on this, so maybe you'd like to give that a read?

I'm at least glad it conveyed the feelings I wanted it to convey! Thanks for reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As much as this makes me say 'big mood', it feels…too perfunctory I think? I'm not sure how it could be fleshed out more but it really feels like it needs more meat to it? Possibly by fleshing out who Kara is as a person a bit more?

The note at the end is weirdly poetic in a way that feels reminiscent of Emily Dickinson but it also feels a touch clunky? Specifically, the 'can' feels like it's too harsh a sounding word for that spot; and the phrase "therefore under these circumstances" feels like it really needs to be a touch more…casual? I think perhaps just the word length is screwing up the meter but I'm not a poet so shrugs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's quite the power, willing yourself out of existence because you can't even XD hearty +1 really creepy and simple!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm in love with the Site-17 deep-well catalogue, and am always glad when a new article is added there. But this? Doesn't do it for me. It seems to be little more than a kind of funny final line.

-1 for now.

Also why did you screw with the formatting here? It's not very significant here, but it also serves no purpose. Why not just use the normal format?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see this one hit the mainlist. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, basically, Dr. Kara can’t even right now?

I actually snorted. I'm very glad that you enjoyed this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, basically, Dr. Kara can’t even right now?

Jokes aside, I did like the feeling of this article. Pulled me out of lurker-land, at the very least.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I cant not upvote this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, Jak! It was fun getting to use an esoteric measurement, thank you for inventing them!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome job! I'm glad I could help you with this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made me smile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you wouldn't mind! It's not very connected to Deepwell, but I tried to pull from the same themes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuck it I'll upvote. Did you want to feature this on the Deepwell hub/tag it, btw?

item.title: Re: Indeed
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Your praise is especially meaningful, lol.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Recommended listening: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jNrtbl6ddZM

This is my second SCP, and my first solo SCP! I'm supremely excited to share this with the site. Writing this was a unique experience, as I got a majority of my crit from the SCP Declassified Discord server, which I've only joined recently.

Firstly, thanks to all the people here and on SCPD that read this through and streamlined it. Those individuals are christopoulos, Ultimate Ridley, ManinDark, gekkoguy, BIRDCHURCH, DoctorMatthews, Its a Bad Idea, JakdragonX (the inventor of olf values), Dr Remember, and DrUnoriginal.

Secondly, an even bigger thanks to KaraKatt, who was practically a co-author. She was the main inspiration behind this piece, and the last quote at the end is something she said in the server, which still leaves me kinda giggly.

Also check out her amazing art of Kass here: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/kara-s-page-of-art

Finally, thanks to you for reading!

item.title: Indeed
item.content:encoded:
Indeed.

(Though I actually really like it. good job.)
